channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Breaking: Paedophile former magistrate to be released on home detention
item.description: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy will be released on strict home detention conditions when his sentence expires next month.
item.dc:creator: Jordanna Schriever

media:group.media:description: Notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy was jailed in 2001 for the sexual abuse of four children.

item.title: Bingo player awarded $150k after tripping on cracked tile at Canberra club
item.description: The woman, who was awarded more than $150,000, rated her pain as 10 out of 10 and spent three nights in hospital.
item.dc:creator: Zaarkacha Marlan

media:group.media:description: The woman fell at the Hellenic Club of Canberra in Phillip in November 2020.

item.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise
item.description: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange, Callum Liddelow, Tim Little, and Eugene Boisvert

media:group.media:description: Health officials are urging the public to get vaccinated against diphtheria, particularly those in high-risk communities.

item.title: Alcaraz injury woes deepen, former champion out of Wimbledon
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz will miss Wimbledon as he continues his rehab from a wrist injury that has already ruled him out of the French Open.

media:group.media:description: Carlos Alcaraz is not ready to take to the court yet, aiming to return for the US hardcourt season.

item.title: Death of Peter Hollingworth sparks difficult feelings for abuse victim
item.description: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth, who resigned over his handling of child abuse in the church, died in Melbourne on Tuesday at the age of 91.
item.dc:creator: Ned Hammond and Will Murray

media:group.media:description: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth died on Tuesday, aged 91.

item.title: Australia's costliest disaster recovery program fails to build single home
item.description: A new report finds a disaster recovery program set up after the devastating 2022 floods in northern NSW is yet to deliver any of the homes it promised.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ross

media:group.media:description: More than 2,000 homes across northern NSW were declared uninhabitable after devastating floods hit the region in early 2022.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese meets family of Kumanjayi Little Baby
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia stands with the family of a five-year-old Aboriginal girl, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs last month.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Dick

media:group.media:description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has comforted Kumanjayi Little Baby's family in Alice Springs.

item.title: Pensioner offered cash for amputation weeks before death, court told
item.description: Witnesses have given evidence about a 66-year-old Queensland man shopping around for someone in his town to amputate his leg in the weeks before he died at a public park.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Testa

media:group.media:description: The court heard John Yalu's co-workers told him not to accept Kalman Tal's offer.

item.title: Teenaged trio arrested over Melbourne nightclub arsons
item.description: Victoria Police allege the three males are responsible for arson attacks at nightclubs La Di Da and Electric Bar earlier this month.

media:group.media:description: Police have arrested three people today over alleged arson attacks at Melbourne nightlife venues.

item.title: Maroons hit with triple injury blow as Blues look for whitewash
item.description: Injuries to three key players have compounded Queensland's task to avoid a 3-0 women's State of Origin whitewash.

media:group.media:description: Queensland skipper Tamika Upton is out of Origin III.

item.title: Diphtheria is spreading in Australia and so is misinformation about it
item.description: Falsehoods about diphtheria are spreading online as a worsening outbreak of the disease affects Indigenous communities.
item.dc:creator: Lucy Carter

media:group.media:description: Diphtheria is spreading in Australia, but so are falsehoods about the highly contagious disease.

item.title: Local in Melbourne's north says recycling plant odour 'a form of torture'
item.description: A urban planning expert says Victoria's industrial zoning laws are outdated and that operations like Visy's paper recycling plant in Reservoir may no longer be appropriate so close to housing.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Brown

media:group.media:description: Residents say the odour emitted from the Visy paper recycling facility in Reservoir leaves them trapped inside their homes.

item.title: Core Lithium restarts Northern Territory mine as price rallies
item.description: Core Lithium has restarted mining at its project near Darwin as prices surge close to $4,200 a tonne.
item.dc:creator: Tom Robinson

media:group.media:description: Core Lithium's Grants deposit processing site near Darwin where mining has restarted after a two-year closure.

item.title: Man who subjected surfer to 'acts of humiliation' jailed over killing
item.description: Guy Haymes died after being assaulted in a dispute over $50 at a Manly apartment in February 2024.
item.dc:creator: Sean Tarek Goodwin

media:group.media:description: Guy Haymes died after being assaulted inside a Manly apartment.

item.title: NT Police officer acquitted of coercing ex-partner over T-shirt assault
item.description: A Northern Territory Police officer has been cleared of coercing his former partner into withdrawing an assault charge after facing trial on one count of attempting to pervert the course of justice.
item.dc:creator: Roxanne Fitzgerald

media:group.media:description: Karol Jarentowski has been acquitted by a Supreme Court jury of attempting to pervert the course of justice.

item.title: South Sydney star retires from rugby league due to MND diagnosis
item.description: Rabbitohs and Maroons enforcer Jai Arrow announces he will step away from rugby league after being diagnosed with motor neurone disease.

media:group.media:description: Jai Arrow was "not in a position to speak" in the club's press conference.

item.title: Doctor fined for book of 'WTF' surgery pictures taken without consent
item.description: An anaesthetist who worked at Toowoomba Hospital has been fined $30,000 for professional misconduct after he included confidential patient information in a self-published book.
item.dc:creator: Elly Bradfield, Aisling Brennan, and Dan McCray

media:group.media:description: Lachlan Rathie has beeen fined $30,000 for professional misconduct.

item.title: Three Aussies in Giro d'Italia top 10 after key time trial
item.description: Ben O'Connor uses his Perth experience to impress in the Giro d'Italia's only time trial stage of 2026, leapfrogging fellow West Australian Jai Hindley in the overall standings, with another Perth boy Michael Storer just behind them.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Ben O'Connor was the third-best-performing GC rider on the time trial stage.

item.title: Mosque shooting victims hailed as heroes and mourned by community
item.description: Three people killed by two shooters at a San Diego mosque were beloved pillars of the community and died while saving 140 children in the building at the time of the attack, authorities say.

media:group.media:description: The three victims of a shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego have been honoured for their heroic actions as the attack unfolded.

item.title: Worm living in a 'glass castle' among thousands of new marine species
item.description: Thousands of species have been discovered lurking and thriving in our oceans, as part of an effort to catalogue 100,000 new species.
item.dc:creator: Fergus Gregg

media:group.media:description: Discovered on a volcanic seamount, this worm makes its home inside a "glass castle": the intricate chambers of a glass sponge.

item.title: Government businesses defy premier's call for transparency
item.description: Tasmanian government-owned businesses or companies are being threatened with legislation to force them to disclose right-to-information documents publicly after nine of them failed to respond to the premier's request.
item.dc:creator: Lucy MacDonald

media:group.media:description: Premier Jeremy Rockliff wrote to government businesses in April to ask them to make disclosure logs.

item.title: Man charged with rape after alleged Adelaide Hills home invasion
item.description: A man who allegedly broke into a home and sexually assaulted the occupant will face court charged with aggravated serious criminal trespass and rape.

media:group.media:description: Police are looking for a vehicle used by a man who allegedly raped a person at Mount Pleasant.

item.title: Investigation stalemate sparks $1m reward for clues in 1995 cold case
item.description: More than 30 years on, mystery still surrounds the death of opal miner Paul Murray. NSW Police hope the offer of a $1 million reward will breathe new life into the investigation.
item.dc:creator: Lily Plass

media:group.media:description: Paul Murray lived on a campsite about 8 kilometres north-west of Lightning Ridge.

item.title: Outbreak of highly contagious infection grows to more than 220 cases
item.description: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Dalzell

media:group.media:description: There was a large spike in flu cases in Queensland children in May 2022.

item.title: Apprehension as UK mining company enters Australian coal
item.description: Anglo American has operated metallurgical coal mines in Queensland since the turn of the century. Now, a UK-based company has bought them all.
item.dc:creator: Liam O'Connell, Yasmine Wright Gittins, and Bryn Wakefield

media:group.media:description: Anglo American has sold five coal mines in Queensland.
